dependency_order.taught: self-contained motivation; seeds all later sections
hashes, one-way scalar-point math, signatures, KEM

assumes: KEM concept deferred: classical DH handshake introduced as black box here

dependency_order.taught: rings, decoys, key images; forward-pointer to cluster tags (S9) is flagged explicitly as a Botho-specific notion taught later
Pedersen hiding/binding/homomorphism, balance equation, range proofs
ML-KEM-768 stealth swap, ML-DSA-65 minting, data-lifetime rule
quorum slices, quorums, intersection, nominate/ballot/externalize, halt-not-fork
proposal-vs-finality split, mining weight != consensus weight, RandomX, adaptive timing pointer to S9
emission schedule, cluster tags, factor, progressive fees, demurrage, burn+lottery, adversarial-sim rationale

assumes: everything S2-S9; no new mechanisms introduced

cross_referenced: WP 2 Related Work (Monero/MobileCoin/Zcash comparison, PQ ring-signature size survey)
WP 3 Preliminaries (Pedersen, Bulletproofs, ML-KEM/ML-DSA parameter facts)
WP 4 Cryptography (key hierarchy, PQ stealth protocol, CLSAG, hybrid rationale table)
WP 5 Transactions (cluster tags, blending, decay, factor curve, Sybil theorem, decoy selection, ring-max fee rule)
WP 6 Consensus (SCP phases, quorum defs, fork-freedom, mining pools)
WP 7 Monetary (emission, tail, dynamic timing, fee split, lottery mechanism, constants)
WP appendix Parameter Justification (ring size, constants rationale)
deliberately_not_duplicated: All security proofs/theorem statements (IND-CCA2 reduction, CLSAG games, fork-freedom proof, Sybil-resistance induction, Nash equilibria) - taught as one-line intuitions, pointed to spec
CLSAG verification equations and aggregation coefficients
SLIP-10/HKDF derivation paths and subaddress equations
The log-sigmoid cluster-factor formula (described qualitatively: midpoint 100k BTH -> 3.5x, clamp 1-6x)
Normative parameter tables (constants quoted selectively, never as a table restating WP 7 / appendix)
Byte-level transaction structures and wire formats (WP 5, WP 8)
Supply projection table and tail-emission algebra

technical_accuracy: Paint-mixing analogy for scalar mult: lossy-but-true (one-wayness, not full group structure)
Pedersen 'locked box bolted to the transaction': hiding stated as information-theoretic per WP 3; binding as computational
'Key image = serial number unique to the coin but unlinkable': true per WP 4 linkability proof sketch
'~50x PQ ring signature overhead / ~35KB per input': WP 4 + WP 2 figures quoted directly
ML-DSA-65 attributed to MINTING signatures (per WP 4), NOT private spend authorization; refs/issue note said 'spend authorization' - spec followed, flagged for audit
Avoided quoting bit-security levels for ML-KEM-768/ML-DSA-65 (WP 3 gives 192/128-bit quantum figures; primer says 'NIST middle security category' - lossy-but-true, no contradiction)
Tail emission 'about 2% net' with gross-vs-net burn adjustment mentioned qualitatively; 611,010,000 rounded to 'roughly 611 million'
Demurrage 'parking fee accrued by idle concentrated wealth, charged when coins move': WP 7 defines it as a per-transfer stock-level term; phrased as charge-on-move to stay true
'Attack costs the attacker ~a fifth of its position over five years': WP 10 says ~19%
Tag decay 'about an hour at reference pace' = 720 blocks at 5s; noted clock-not-count constraint per WP 5
Lottery: 4 winners/block, dust floor 1 micro-BTH, maturity 720 blocks, seed from previous externalized block, payout capped at one reward - all per WP 7/WP 9
Finality 'a handful of seconds after proposal' = WP 6 block time + ~3-4s
Capstone fee 'a few tens of nano-BTH' for 2-in-2-out: consistent with WP 7 worked 16 nano-BTH (1-in-2-out floor) scaled for a larger ~5KB tx
MobileCoin characterization (SCP without mining, fixed supply, classical-only, SGX omitted as out of scope) per WP 2
